question.text: The NPS Systematic Lump Sum Withdrawal (SLW) facility allows a subscriber to:

answer.text: Withdraw the entire corpus immediately at age 60
Withdraw the non-annuity portion (up to 60%) in periodic installments until age 75
Withdraw from Tier II account only
Withdraw more than 60% of the corpus as lump sum

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The SLW facility allows subscribers to withdraw the lump sum portion (up to 60%) on a periodic basis (monthly, quarterly, etc.) over a period up to age 75, instead of taking it all at once. **The Trap:** SLW does not allow more than 60% withdrawal (Option D). It is a phased withdrawal option for the permissible lump sum, not the annuity portion. **Cross-Exam Context:** SLW is a relatively new NPS feature tested in Series XVII. It combines the benefits of lump sum access with phased withdrawal discipline. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SLW is excellent for retirees who want the flexibility of phased income without committing the lump sum to a low-yield product immediately. The remaining corpus continues to earn market returns.
